evals.name: scope-epics-from-product
evals.prompt: You are a product-strategist agent. Read the product.yaml at core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/fixtures/product.yaml. This is a LOCKED product artifact. Your task: invoke the scope-roadmap-epics skill to derive IDD epics from this product. Each epic must represent a distinct deliverable capability, reference a strategic goal from product.yaml, have IDD fields (intent with p1/p2/p3, constraints, success_scenarios, failure_conditions), and use E-IDs (E1, E2, etc.). Write the output epics to core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/outputs/epics.yaml.
evals.expected_output: A valid epics.yaml with epics, each having E-ID, name, strategic_goal_ref matching an SG from product.yaml, IDD fields (intent.p1/p2/p3, constraints.in_scope/out_of_scope/must_not_break, success_scenarios with given/when/then, failure_conditions), priority, depends_on, foundation flag

assertions.name: epics_are_distinct_capabilities
assertions.description: Each epic represents a distinct, deliverable capability — not task-level or bundling unrelated work

assertions.name: all_epics_have_e_ids
assertions.description: Every epic has an id field matching E[0-9]+ pattern

assertions.name: strategic_goal_refs_valid
assertions.description: Every epic's strategic_goal_ref matches an SG-ID in product.yaml (SG1-SG5)

assertions.name: idd_fields_complete
assertions.description: Every epic has intent.p1, intent.p2, intent.p3, constraints.in_scope, constraints.out_of_scope, constraints.must_not_break, success_scenarios (>=2), failure_conditions (>=2)

assertions.name: no_f_ids
assertions.description: No epic uses F-ID pattern — epics must use E-IDs only

evals.name: draft-roadmap-yaml-from-brief
evals.prompt: You are a product-strategist agent. Read the product.yaml at core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/fixtures/product.yaml, the epics at core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/outputs/epics.yaml, and the feasibility at core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/outputs/feasibility.yaml. Assume the roadmap brief has been Tether-approved. Your task: invoke the draft-roadmap skill to produce roadmap.yaml. The output must conform to the schema at core/components/skills/draft-roadmap/schemas/roadmap.yaml. Write to core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/outputs/roadmap.yaml.
evals.expected_output: A valid roadmap.yaml with: slug, status DRAFT, product_ref, approved_brief_ref, thesis (1-2 sentences), narrative (3-4 paragraphs), timeline with near/mid/long horizons referencing E-IDs, feasibility per epic, critical_blockers, open_questions, risk_summary, exclusions, and assumptions.

assertions.name: status_is_draft
assertions.description: roadmap.yaml has status: DRAFT

assertions.name: has_thesis
assertions.description: thesis field is non-empty string

assertions.name: timeline_has_3_horizons
assertions.description: timeline has entries for near, mid, and long horizons

assertions.name: epic_refs_are_e_ids
assertions.description: All epic_refs in timeline and feasibility use E-ID pattern

assertions.name: feasibility_per_epic
assertions.description: Every epic referenced in timeline has a corresponding feasibility entry

assertions.name: conforms_to_schema
assertions.description: Output has all top-level fields from roadmap.yaml schema: slug, status, product_ref, thesis, narrative, timeline, feasibility, critical_blockers, open_questions, risk_summary, exclusions, assumptions

assertions.name: no_tech_jargon_in_thesis
assertions.description: Thesis does not contain implementation-level technology names (React, Python, PostgreSQL, etc.)

assertions.name: brief_content_preserved
assertions.description: thesis, narrative, timeline sequencing, exclusions, and assumptions are copied from the approved brief or locked product artifact rather than regenerated

evals.name: roadmap-brief-phoenix-sidebar
evals.prompt: Convert the product.yaml at core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/fixtures/product.yaml and the epics at core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/outputs/epics.yaml to JSON, then copy the roadmap-brief.html template to produce the brief. The brief must use the Phoenix Design System sidebar + chapters layout with Strategy, Timeline, Epics, and Feasibility chapters plus visible Tether/Vanish/Orbit review actions. Write to core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/outputs/roadmap-brief.html.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained HTML file with: Phoenix CSS tokens, sidebar navigation for Strategy/Timeline/Epics/Feasibility, theme switcher, visible Tether/Vanish/Orbit review actions, and no inline comment system.

assertions.name: is_self_contained_html
assertions.description: Output is valid HTML5 with no external CDN links or script src references

assertions.name: has_sidebar_chapters
assertions.description: HTML contains sidebar navigation entries for Strategy, Timeline, Epics, and Feasibility

assertions.name: phoenix_tokens
assertions.description: CSS contains Phoenix tokens --bg-primary: #1A2332 and --color-water: #00D4FF

assertions.name: theme_switcher_present
assertions.description: HTML contains theme switcher controls for Dark, Light, and Neon themes

assertions.name: review_actions_present
assertions.description: Sidebar exposes Tether, Vanish, and Orbit review action buttons

assertions.name: no_inline_comment_system
assertions.description: HTML and JavaScript do not contain inline comment popup, localStorage comment persistence, or a comments tab

evals.name: reverse-coverage-scope-and-deferred-items
evals.prompt: You are a judge agent performing a reverse-coverage check. Read product.yaml at core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/fixtures/product.yaml and epics.yaml at core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/outputs/epics.yaml. Check: (1) Every item in product.yaml scope.in_scope maps to at least one epic's in_scope as explicit ownership — no capability silently absorbed as implementation detail. (2) No epic's in_scope contains a capability from product.yaml scope.out_of_scope or deferred items. (3) Any cross-cutting capabilities have cross_cutting_justification in their epic. Write coverage report to core/components/plays/plan-roadmap/evals/outputs/coverage-check.yaml.
evals.expected_output: A coverage-check.yaml with per-item status (covered/orphaned_scope/deferred_violation), scope_item_coverage section listing each in_scope item and its owning epic, deferred_violations section (should be empty), and summary counts

assertions.name: scope_items_all_covered
assertions.description: Every in_scope item from product.yaml has status covered — no orphaned_scope entries

assertions.name: no_deferred_violations
assertions.description: No epic's in_scope contains a capability from product.yaml out_of_scope — deferred_violations list is empty

assertions.name: cross_cutting_justified
assertions.description: Any epic that spans multiple modules has a cross_cutting_justification field explaining the exception to Rule 2

assertions.name: coverage_report_complete
assertions.description: Report contains scope_item_coverage, deferred_violations, and summary sections with counts
